fear: That he will be ruined financially regardless of how tonight ends — he has already petitioned Governor Hutchinson for a pass to sail the tea back to England and been refused.
name: Captain John Rotch
role: Owner of the Dartmouth, the tea ship at Griffin's Wharf
voice: Exhausted and plaintive, with the cadence of a man who has been awake for three days — he sighs between sentences and frequently trails off as though he has given up on finishing his own thoughts.
secrets: He kept the red-wax-sealed document he found on his gangplank — he did not know what to do with it and stuffed it into a cargo crate on the dock.
He has already decided that if the mob boards his ship tonight, he will not resist — he simply cannot afford to.
knowledge: He saw a man matching Greenleaf's description speaking with a customs officer near the Customs Dock earlier this afternoon.
He knows that a document was passed between two men near his ship's gangplank — he did not read it but noticed it was folded in a distinctive way, sealed with red wax.
He is aware that customs men have been placed on alert tonight, which he finds suspicious given that the information about the boarding plan was supposed to be secret.
publicFace: A harried, exhausted merchant caught between the Patriots who threaten him if he unloads the tea and the customs men who threaten him if he does not — he wants only to be left alone.
represents: REQUIRES FACT-CHECK — verify relationship to Francis Rotch (documented owner of the Dartmouth tea ship)
trustLogic: He opens up to anyone who seems sympathetic to his impossible position and does not demand he take a side. He shuts down if he believes the conversation will get him in trouble with either the Patriots or the Crown.
introAnchor: the owner of the Dartmouth, caught between Crown authority and Patriot threat with no good options and an exhaustion that shows in every line of his face
privateGoal: He wants the standoff resolved without his ship being destroyed and without him being held personally liable by either side.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: He throws his hands up and insists he is just a merchant, not a politician, not a spy, not a hero.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not formally testify against anyone — he is too afraid of retaliation from both sides.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: He becomes agitated and starts pacing, saying repeatedly that he wants no part of any of this.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: He sits down heavily and tells the player everything he knows — he is too tired to pretend anymore.
